steps.title: Read the exponential form
steps.content: The model is $$f(t)=60{,}000\cdot 2^{t/410}.$$ The $60{,}000$ is the starting amount. The factor $2^{t/410}$ tells how many doublings have occurred.

steps.title: Set one doubling
steps.content: One doubling means the multiplier should be $2$, so the exponent on base $2$ must be $1$: $$\frac{t}{410}=1.$$

steps.title: Solve and verify
steps.content: Solving gives $t=410$. At $t=410$, the model becomes $60{,}000\cdot 2^1=120{,}000$, exactly double the initial population. The answer is 410.
